title: Capstone 07 — End-to-End Fine-Tuning Pipeline (Data to SFT to DPO to Serve)

questions.question: Why does the pipeline compose SFT then DPO (or GRPO) rather than DPO alone?
options: SFT establishes domain behavior on labeled completions while DPO or GRPO aligns the model against preference pairs or verifiable rewards
DPO requires a separate base model architecture
DPO cannot run on quantized weights
questions.explanation: SFT establishes domain behavior on labeled completions while DPO or GRPO aligns the model against preference pairs or verifiable rewards as described in the pipeline stages.

questions.question: What does EAGLE-3 contribute to the vLLM serving stage?
options: An OPA policy for tool calls
Draft heads that predict N tokens ahead; the target verifies in one pass for 2-3x throughput
questions.explanation: Axolotl is used because it provides pre-built configurations for SFT and DPO, supports LoRA/QLoRA for efficient fine-tuning, and integrates with vLLM for serving.

questions.question: Which metric reports how well a speculative-decoding draft aligns with the target model?
questions.explanation: EAGLE-3 reduces inference latency by generating multiple tokens per draft step and verifying them in parallel, achieving 2-3x speedup on the served model.

questions.question: Which trio of quants does the pipeline ship for deployment flexibility?
questions.explanation: The pipeline stages are: data preparation (cleaning and deduplication), SFT (domain fine-tuning), DPO/GRPO (preference alignment), and vLLM serving (optimized inference).

questions.question: Which framework convention does the 2026 model card follow in this capstone?
options: Model Openness Framework (MOF) 2026 template covering data, training, eval, safety, license, and reproducibility
questions.explanation: The capstone measures training efficiency (tokens/sec), alignment quality (reward model score on held-out preferences), and serving throughput (requests/sec at target latency).

questions.question: Which HPA metric is used to autoscale the serving replicas?
questions.explanation: Robustness is tested with data quality tests (checking for toxic content in training data), overfitting checks (evaluating on held-out datasets), and serving stability tests (long-running load tests).
